name: Google Blogger REST API
what_it_does: Google's hosted blogging platform REST API — enabling developers to programmatically manage Blogger blogs, create and publish posts, manage comments, manage pages, and retrieve blog content, with OAuth2 authentication for private operations and API key access for public blog data, supporting the free Google-hosted Blogger.com platform used by millions of bloggers.
use_cases: Publishing blog posts programmatically via Blogger API for automated content publishing workflows
Retrieving blog content via Blogger API for content aggregation and republishing
Managing blog comments via Blogger API for moderation automation
Migrating blog content via Blogger API for platform transitions
Building Blogger analytics dashboards via Blogger API to track posts and engagement
not_for: Enterprise CMS with complex workflows (use WordPress or Contentful for enterprise CMS)
E-commerce or product-focused sites (Blogger is blog-focused only)
Teams needing custom domain control without Google (Blogger requires Google account ownership)
best_when: A developer needs to programmatically manage an existing Blogger.com blog or build simple content publishing automation for Google's free blogging platform.
avoid_when: You need a modern CMS with rich plugins, headless CMS capabilities, or enterprise content workflows — Blogger is a legacy free platform with limited customization.

auth.notes: OAuth2 for write operations (post creation, comment management). API key for public read-only operations. OAuth2 scopes: https://www.googleapis.com/auth/blogger (full access) and https://www.googleapis.com/auth/blogger.readonly (read-only). Standard Google OAuth2 flow via Google Identity Platform. Tokens via Authorization: Bearer header. No webhooks — polling required.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Completely free — Google Blogger platform and API have no cost
pricing.notes: Google product (originally Pyra Labs, acquired 2003). Launched 2004. Free hosting on blogspot.com subdomain or custom domain. API v3 released 2011. Quota: 10,000 requests/day (generous). Legacy platform — Google has not heavily developed Blogger since ~2015. Still maintained but not a growth priority for Google. 500M+ blogs historically. Google Drive integration for image storage. No comments API for Blogger's native comment system (Disqus often used). Blogger templates for theming. Very stable API — minimal breaking changes since v3 launch.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Google REST API v3. OAuth2 + API key. Standard Google API conventions. 10,000 req/day quota. Free. Legacy platform — stable but not evolving. Excellent Google developer documentation. Standard Google error codes.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Post reads idempotent by postId. Blog reads idempotent by blogId. Post creation: not idempotent — check for existing posts before creating. Update (PUT) operations are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth2 required for all write operations — API key alone is read-only for public blogs
Blog ID (not URL) is required for all operations — use /blogs/byurl to resolve URL to ID
No webhooks — must poll for new comments or post updates
Legacy platform — Blogger has limited development investment from Google since ~2015
Daily quota: 10,000 requests/day — shared across all operations for a project
Post status: LIVE vs DRAFT vs SCHEDULED — publishing requires explicit status=LIVE

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced (Google infrastructure). OAuth2 with granular scopes (full vs read-only). Google security posture. GDPR compliance. Google data privacy policies apply. Blog content stored on Google servers (US). No self-hosted option. OAuth2 scope minimization: use readonly scope when possible.
